description: Synthetic HL7 v2.4 ORU^R01 QC-mode upload from an EDAN H60S 3-part hematology analyzer (MLLP; the analyzer is the TCP client, our edge listens), MSH-16=1 (QC per KB §3.2.1). Exercises the EDAN QC OBR layout (KB §3.2.3: PID omitted; OBR-2=QC file No., OBR-3=QC level digit, OBR-4=<facility>^<model>^QC, OBR-13=QC lot No.) and the LIS-110 vendor-aware result-type/QC-layout gate.

source.reference: Synthetic, manual-derived from EDAN\WI\82-01.54.460907 KB §3.2.1 (0/empty=sample, 1=QC) and §3.2.3 (QC OBR layout: PID-less, OBR-2=QC file No., OBR-3=level, OBR-13=lot, OBR-4 ^QC). LIS-110.
source.note: NO real QC-mode bench capture exists yet — the 2026-07-06/07 physical H60S bench (LIS-20; docs/runbooks/edan-h60s-bench-conformance.md) observed only MSH-16=0 (patient-mode results), =2 (MSH-only connection-test ping), and =3 (host-query QRY^R02); MSH-16=1 (KB-documented QC) was never produced on the wire, so this fixture's OBR-13/OBR-3 QC layout and its representative control values are manual/KB-derived, not bench-corroborated. A future QC-mode bench run must confirm MSH-16=1 and the exact §3.2.3 OBR field positions, or amend the profile (see the runbook's LIS-110 AC-5 deviation note). CBC codes/units/OBX layout are otherwise copied from the graduated edan-h60s-oru-r01 patient fixture (real EDANLAB wire shape: OBX-4 analyte code, OBX-3 suspect flag placeholder, HL7-escaped units, trailing OBX-12 value^unit component the parser ignores).

terminology.note: EDAN CBC analyte codes/units mirrored from the default RAC-050 seed map that edan-h60s-oru-r01 relies on implicitly, so this QC fixture proves its own code mapping per the Normalizer.from_fixture contract.
